meaning: vt.阉割,使柔弱,删削,使无力adj.阉割了的,柔弱的
examples: To deprive of virility or spirit; emasculate.
But God, He is apparently very sturdy, but why He always makes people feel that He is so emasculate.
The dodgy recruitment drive is the latest phase of a campaign to emasculate Mr Yushchenko, which began at the start of the unlikely cohabitation of the two Viktors.
Dr. Pascale suggests that retirement can actually emasculate men, particularly when their wife continues working, by stripping them of their role as bread winner.
They believe that many unionists have never had any intention of sharing power, and are using decommissioning either to break the peace deal, or to emasculate the republican movement.
